comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** There was no prior solution.tex; I wrote a complete, verified solution to problem appliedmath_rm14k (= Problem 10, Preconditioned CG for the RKHS-constrained CP mode subproblem, Kolda--Ward).

**What was established (all numerically verified in workspace):**

1. **Exact structural identities** (Lemmas 1--2, Props 1--2) reducing every operation to the small/observed data:
   - Model eval: `S^T(Z⊗K)vec(W) = m`, `m_ℓ = (KW)[i_ℓ,:]·Z̃[ℓ,:]`.
   - Adjoint scatter: `(Z⊗K)^T S y = vec(K (R_y Z))` with `R_y Z = Σ_ℓ y_ℓ e_{i_ℓ} Z̃[ℓ,:]`.
   - **Matrix-free matvec**: `H vec(W) = vec(K P + λ K W)`, `P = Σ_ℓ m_ℓ e_{i_ℓ} Z̃[ℓ,:]`, depending only on K (n×n), Z̃ (q×r), and the row indices {i_ℓ} — never O(M) or O(N). Verified to 1e-13.
   - MTTKRP `B = TZ = Σ_ℓ t_ℓ e_{i_ℓ} Z̃[ℓ,:]` and RHS `g = vec(KB)`, both from observed data only.

2. **Singular-kernel handling** (Lemmas 3--4): proved `null(H) = I_r ⊗ null(K)` and `range(H) = range(I_r⊗K) ∋ g`, so the system is consistent; CG from x_0=0 stays in range(H) and returns the unique min-norm solution. Verified with a rank-deficient K: solution had zero nullspace component, residual 6e-12.

3. **Preconditioner** `P_c = (Z̃^T Z̃ + λ I_r) ⊗ K` (Prop 4): SPD on range(H), shares null(H), and its (pseudo)inverse applies as `vec(K^+ G C^{-1})` via one n×n eigendecomposition and one r×r factorization — no O(M). Verified condition-number reduction ~100–160× across tests.

4. **Both required algorithm boxes**: direct symmetric solve (`H = Φ^T Φ + λ(I_r⊗K)`, Φ rows = Z̃[ℓ,:]⊗K̃[ℓ,:], cost O(q n²r² + n³r³)) and matrix-free PCG (setup O(n³+qr²); per-iter O(n²r+nr²+qr)). Termination, correctness, complexity, and memory O(n²+qr) all proved.

End-to-end PCG run reached relative residual <1e-8 in fewer iterations than plain CG. LaTeX environments/math all balanced (no toolchain installed to compile, checked manually).

The constraint "avoid any O(M)/O(N) computation" is satisfied throughout: Z appears only through its observed rows Z̃, and the kernel only as the small K (or K̃ in the direct assembly).
